text: A very interesting method. Thank you 👍💪✌

author: @dannystrachan475
text: Strong and also free balls could be sourced from empty anti perspirant roll on containers.

author: @aurigo_tech
text: For visibility reasons in the video I would recommend to print the different pieces of such models in darker and different colors. All white is not ideal.

author: @marcfruchtman9473
text: Thanks for this video. I see what you are saying about this having some limits, as it seems like the points of contact are limited.

author: @mitchpayson6203
text: 😀 i was so excited, for years walking my dog past a golf course has given me allot of balls. golf ball is bigger 40mm and 42.6.. will they fit in and give a different ratio..

author: @marjon1703
text: Made me giggle, explaining a not well known engineering gizmo with ping-pong balls! Brilliant!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @denverbraughler3948
text: Did you say “continuously variable transmission”?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes have a look at the NuVinci transmission

author: @russellzauner
text: Enviolo makes a CVT for bicycles; this is a very similar design.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

replies.author: @kinchan3334
replies.text: Yeah and they have plenty of videos online about how the system works.

author: @michaelsohocki1573
text:
Rob, what would a gearshift look like for this apparatus? I'm trying to picture it on a bicycle or some other simple illustration of function. If the degree of compression is the magic that makes it all work without destroying itself, How would you adjust the height/distance to ply a different part  of the balls, while maintaining really really constant pressure? 

(The precision would have to be out of this world.)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to make it a bicycle transmission you would swivel the ball axis on pivot pins and linkages mate

author: @maxkennedy5073
text: Looks like the basis for some bicycle CVT's I've seen such as Nuvinci.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is

author: @d.marbus1493
text: Look into Van Doorne transmission aka Variomatic. I'm sure you'll like it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i know that transmission type  mate - but thanks fr thinking of me

author: @jessedutton3112
text: The lack of contrast makes the model less clear on video. Different filament colors for moving parts would be helpful.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: do you know what mate - doesn't matter what you do - someone will suggest something different - i like white it shows up fine - multi- colour would make it look like a pop tart.

author: @velcroman11
text: This mechanism was used by General Motors in the steering boxes.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oh nice one - cheers mate

author: @snowstrobe
text: So is this the basic idea behind the so called 'infinite' (continuously variable) bike hub gears Enviolo make?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yeah same idea - you just swivel the balls around so the contact diameter is different and you have a CVT

replies.author: @snowstrobe
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  Fascinating stuff!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @chrishill1219
text: Facilitating Robert.  The Hayes gearbox that was fitted to Austins around 1930 I believe was the same principal and if I remember correctly it was wear that finished them off.  I believe there's an Australian that has one in his car and rebuilt it using better materials and oils. Love all the subjects you come up with. Thank you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome mate - thanks for the info - i love when folks add to the vid thanks for adding

author: @stewartpalmer2456
text: Is there anyway you would be willing to show 3 examples of the maths involved here. i.e. Upper diameter, middle diameter and lower diameter on the ball bearings? Could it work with a thrust bearing setup?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well i can do the math - i just it will be watched by you and me lol

replies.author: @stewartpalmer2456
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering All education is worth something. Cheers mate.

author: @clickbaitnumberone1403
text: good one

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @runowood6810
text: Do you think spherical gears or some of those other new multiaxis gears could improve it?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am not sure what you mean by improve mate - one of the design objectives here was cost - multi axis gears would make it very much more expensive so in that sense it wouldn't improve it - it would increase the torque capability so in that sense it would improve it

replies.author: @runowood6810
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  very well said. The design goal of low cost would be nullified to an extent. Keep up the awesome work! Your channel has been priceless and your attiude has been an inspiration.

author: @alvalent8674
text: ratio looks to be 24 or 25 to 1

replies.author: @isaacwalters747
replies.text: I think it's either 25 or 6 to 4...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @flightschoolcustomfootwear
text: I love your channel and all of your videos. Thank you for all of your effort. I have some blueprints to build what is called an Infinity Coil, but i am not well versed in electical work. I know i can make it, but i am not sure how to power it after it is built. I was wondering if youd be interested in building and testing it. It involes some 3D printing, which i see you are great at. Id love to send the files your way. I have them in CAD format already too

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am most sorry mate I wouldn't do that I am afraid - I get lots and lots of requests to do what you are asking and it is just impossible for me to do - so all i can suggest is you try and get to grips with it

replies.author: @flightschoolcustomfootwear
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  no worries at all. Thanks for letting me know. Cant hurt to ask. I will get working on it soon and let you know how it goes :)

replies.author: @_Bear70
replies.text: @flightschoolcustomfootwear For what its worth, chat gpt and an electricians reference book got me pretty well versed on stuff to let me to electrical projects

replies.author: @flightschoolcustomfootwear
replies.text: @_Bear70  damn that's a great idea! I'm gonna check that out. Thank you!!

author: @grendel1960a
text: love it., I have been busy today playing with my new tool, when I saw a near half price offer for halloween on a 40W diode laser cutter/ engraver, I bit the bullet and got it, I have spent the day cutting out box sides with box joints, love the accuracy and repeatability (and speed) I could achieve the same reult with other tools, but the cuts it makes are so precise and clean (well worth putting up / dealing with the smoke generated)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome mate - 40W has some oomph so it will be a great cutter - they are almost addictive aren't they lol

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Nice device!  Guessing, I get that the ratio is near 15:1.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's not bad from ping pong balls! lol - cheers mate

replies.author: @azlandpilotcar4450
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  Golfballs are about the same size, and can handle more friction and stress.  I also won't get flashbacks to Captain Kangaroo!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @azlandpilotcar4450 that's a great idea mate - ping pong balls have something else going for them too - they act like springs - I haven't mentioned it yet but it is an important point - i will be expanding on this in the next vid

replies.author: @Patriarchtech
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Golf balls are very "springy" too  - The inside is usually or used to be made of cork. (not that I am a golf expert by any means, but when you see a golfer hit the ball in super slow with a driver, then it actually deforms to an egg shape momentarily ) If you need compression golf balls might yield a better result - I think this is a very good suggestion.

author: @rifattarkyararbas4058
text: Thank you very much...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: You're most welcome
